OUESTION
Does Planning Corunissioner Erickson have a conflict of

CONCLUSION
The planning conuissioner has no disqualifying economic

FACTS
Planning Comraissioner Janes Erickson was appointed to the San
Juan capistrano Planning coniission on November 21, 1989.
Conmissioner Erickson's spouse is a former ernployee of the san
Juan capistrano RedeveJ'oprnent Agency and her business currently
perfor:ui services as an independent contractor for the
iedevelopnent agency. According to your facts, Ms. Erickson's
business- is a sole proprietorship and aII the incone received fron
the business is retain-d as separate property. You also stated
that the planning conmission was in no way involved in the
granting -or nainienance of the contract r'rith Cornrnissioner
Erickson's spouse.
Under the agreement lrith the redeveLopnent agency,
Ms. Erickson provides analysis, advice, and information to the
redevelopment agency on an rron caII', basis. You stated that under
the agreinent u!. Eiickson has received between S1,0oo to 920,0o0
for htr services. Planning coumissioner Erickson has becoure
concerned that the compensation received by his spouse for
performing services for the redevel'opment agency nay result in
LonfLicts-of interest for the comrnissioner. You have asked
rrhether coumissioner Erickson is disqualified froru voting on land
use applications before the planning coumission involving proposed
projects located in the redevelopnent area.
ANALYSIS
section 87100 of the Act prohibits any pubtic official

has a financial inteiest.3

every natural person who is a nertber, officer, enployee, or

or greater.
Thus, pursuant to section 87103, the ptanning conrnissioner

4 section 82005 defines rrbusiness enti.tytr as any organization or

Uoreover, Section 82030(a) provides that the income of an

Investnent Interests

whether the financial consequences of a decision are reason-
